account.payoutInfo: Automatic payouts are processed every {interval} for balances above {minPayout}.
account.immatureInfo: Immature balance will become pending after blocks are confirmed ({confirmations} confirmations).
account.notFoundDesc: This account has not been seen on the pool yet. Start mining to see your statistics here.
account.invalidAddressDesc: The address format is invalid. Please check and try again.
account.validAddressFormat: A valid Ethereum address must be 42 characters starting with 0x followed by 40 hexadecimal characters.

calculator.title: Mining Calculator
calculator.estimateRewards: Estimate your mining rewards based on your hashrate
calculator.disclaimer: * Estimates based on current network conditions. Actual results may vary.
calculator.disclaimerText: These calculations are estimates based on current network conditions. Actual mining rewards may vary due to luck, network difficulty changes, pool fees, and other factors. This calculator assumes solo mining equivalent rewards.

help.title: Getting Started
help.followSteps: Follow these simple steps to start mining {coinName} with our pool
help.step1Desc: To receive your mining rewards, you'll need a {coinName} wallet. If you don't have one, you can create a secure wallet using MetaMask or other Ethereum-compatible wallets.
help.step2Desc: Use your favorite mining software to connect to our pool. Here are the stratum server details:
help.step3Desc: Once your miner is configured, run it to start mining. You can monitor your hashrate, earnings, and payout status on our dashboard by looking up your wallet address.
help.workerNameDesc: Optional. Use address.worker_name format to track individual rigs.
help.difficultyDesc: Optional. Pool auto-adjusts difficulty. Use address.worker_name+diff to set manually.
help.lolMinerDesc: is our recommended mining software for both Nvidia and AMD GPUs. It offers excellent performance, stability, and supports a wide range of hardware.
help.alternativeSoftwareDesc: You can also use other mining software if you prefer:
help.poolFeeDesc3: on all mining rewards. This helps us maintain and improve the pool infrastructure.
help.paymentBatchInfo: Payments are processed in batches. If your balance is below the minimum threshold, it will accumulate until the next payout cycle.
help.chooseServer: Choose the server closest to your location for optimal mining performance and lower latency.
help.tip1: Use the server closest to your location for best performance
help.tip2: All servers share the same pool and payouts
help.tip3: You can switch between servers at any time without losing progress
help.choosePort: Choose a port based on your mining hardware:
help.faqHashrateZero: Why is my hashrate showing 0 on the dashboard?
help.faqHashrateZeroAnswer: It may take a few minutes for your hashrate to appear after starting mining. The pool calculates your hashrate based on submitted shares, so please wait 5-10 minutes for accurate statistics to display.
help.faqFirstPayout: When will I receive my first payout?
help.faqFirstPayoutAnswer: Payouts are processed every {interval}. Once your balance reaches {minPayout} {symbol}, you will receive a payout in the next payout cycle. You can check your pending balance on the dashboard.
help.faqPortDifference: What is the difference between the ports?
help.faqPortDifferenceAnswer: Different ports have different difficulty settings. Port 8002 (2 GH) is best for lower-end GPUs, Port 8004 (44 GH) is for high-end GPUs, and Port 8009 (999 GH) is optimized for NiceHash rentals. Choose the port that matches your hardware.
help.faqMultipleWorkers: Can I use multiple workers with the same wallet?
help.faqMultipleWorkersAnswer: Yes! You can use multiple workers with the same wallet address. Simply use different worker names (e.g., rig1, rig2) for each miner. All rewards will be accumulated to the same wallet and paid out together.
help.faqWhyLolMinerAnswer: lolMiner is recommended because it supports both Nvidia and AMD GPUs, has a low developer fee (0.7%), offers excellent stability, and is actively maintained. It's also easy to configure and works well with our pool.
help.faqCheckStats: How do I check my mining statistics?
help.faqCheckStatsAnswer: Enter your wallet address in the search box on the dashboard or navigate directly to /account/YOUR_ADDRESS. You can view your hashrate, pending balance, total paid, and individual worker statistics.

faucet.title: Faucet
faucet.howItWorksDesc: Enter your wallet address and click claim to receive free coins. There is a cooldown period between claims.
faucet.comingSoonDesc: The faucet service is being set up. Please check back later.
faucet.faucetDisabledDesc: The faucet is currently not available.
faucet.step1: Connect MetaMask or create a wallet for {coinName} (Chain ID: {chainId})
faucet.step2: Click the wallet button or paste your address (starts with 0x)
faucet.step4: Wait for the transaction to be confirmed (~{blockTime}s)
faucet.warning: This faucet is for testing purposes only. Please don't abuse this service. Excessive requests may result in temporary restrictions.
faucet.metaMaskNotInstalled: MetaMask is not installed. Please install it to use this feature.
faucet.invalidAddress: Please enter a valid Ethereum address (0x followed by 40 hex characters)
faucet.networkError: Network error. Please try again later.
faucet.failedToAddNetwork: Failed to add network to MetaMask

about.title: Terms of Service
about.subtitle: Please read our terms carefully before using the pool
about.overviewDesc: {poolName} (hereafter referred to as "the Pool") is a service designed for decentralized mining, providing users with a high-performance mining environment. By using the Pool, users agree to accept all terms and risks outlined below.
about.experimentalSoftwareDesc: The Pool utilizes software written in Go, designed to be highly concurrent and low in memory consumption. This software is still under development, meaning there may be unexpected bugs or errors. Users must accept all risks associated with using this experimental software.
about.acceptanceOfRiskDesc: Users acknowledge and accept all risks (e.g., hardware failure, network interruption, software bugs) related to the use of the Pool. By using the Pool, users agree not to seek compensation for any irreversible losses.
about.liabilityOfPoolOwnerDesc: The Pool owner will make every effort to prevent the worst-case scenarios, but does not provide compensation for losses arising from inevitable events or issues. Users understand that there are no guarantees provided regarding the Pool's performance.
about.highPerformanceProxyDesc: The Pool utilizes a high-performance proxy server that allows for highly parallel processing and low latency.
about.blockUnlockingDesc: The Pool has a block unlocking and payout module designed to distribute mining rewards fairly.
about.distributedArchitectureDesc: Built on a 100% distributed architecture using multiple servers to ensure high availability and fault tolerance.
about.modernInterfaceDesc: A modern, user-friendly interface built with Next.js for real-time monitoring of mining progress and rewards.
about.fraudulentActivitiesDesc: Users agree not to engage in fraudulent activities such as unfair mining practices, hacking, or spamming. Any detected fraudulent behavior will result in immediate suspension of the user account.
about.personalInfoMisuseDesc: Users agree that their personal information provided during Pool usage will be handled according to the Pool's Privacy Policy.
about.rewardDistributionDesc: The Pool distributes mining rewards proportionally based on the computational resources provided by each user. The calculation method for rewards is available in the official guide and is subject to change.
about.minPayoutThresholdDesc: Users must meet a minimum payout threshold of {minPayout} {symbol} before rewards are distributed. This threshold may be changed at the discretion of the Pool owner.
about.serviceInterruptionDesc: The Pool may experience temporary service interruptions or downtime due to maintenance or unforeseen issues. In such cases, the Pool owner is not liable for any loss incurred by the user.
about.thirdPartyLiabilityDesc: If a user causes damage to a third party, the user will be held responsible, and the Pool owner will not be liable for any damages or legal consequences.
about.changesToTermsDesc: The Pool reserves the right to update or change these Terms of Service at any time. If changes are made, users will be notified through the official website. Continued use of the service after the changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
about.governingLawDesc: These Terms of Service are governed by the laws of Japan. Any disputes arising from the use of this service shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Tokyo District Court.
about.contactInfoDesc: For any inquiries or support requests, please contact us through the following channels:
about.readyToMine: Ready to start mining? Check out our
about.openSourceDesc: This pool software is open source and available on GitHub.
